name: Prune AND Blueberry Leather
intro: If you don’t want to make strips, it can also be nice to use, say, gingerbread men molds to make little figures. Children love this—grown-ups, too. Prunes are very rich in vitamin A and K. And of course, a few prunes a day is excellent for intestinal health.
method: Soak the prunes for about 8 hours. Mix the prunes and the blueberries in a food processor to make a fruit batter.

Spread the batter onto waxed parchment paper, or pour a thin layer in molds. Let dry for about 15 hours. The leathers should be a bit chewy; cut them into strips and roll them up. Keep the leathers in an airtight jar.
